title: Data, API, Algorithm, and Domain Invariants · Product Reasoning Brief: Runtime Admission
summary: The product has schemas and endpoints but no proof of identity, authorization, concurrency, numerical, algorithmic, lifecycle, or compatibility correctness. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone runtime admission increment in executable domain and interface assurance suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use.
Formalize critical identities, states, quantities, relationships, transitions, and impossible conditions in domain language.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Runtime Admission · Theory Runtime Admission
steps.instruction:
theory case — Schemas and endpoints look complete while identity, authorization, concurrency, numerical, lifecycle, compatibility, and recovery invariants remain implicit. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Runtime Admission contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Runtime Admission · Theory Runtime Admission.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use. For Predict Runtime Admission · Theory Runtime Admission, keep Runtime Admission connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Schemas and endpoints look complete while identity, authorization, concurrency, numerical, lifecycle, compatibility, and recovery invariants remain implicit. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Runtime Admission condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GitHub profile and project presentation documentation — Controls selective project presentation, useful README evidence, setup, demonstration, testing, dependency currency, and truthful portfolio communication.

content.text: Validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Schemas and endpoints look complete while identity, authorization, concurrency, numerical, lifecycle, compatibility, and recovery invariants remain implicit. The worked example variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use. A defensible solution applies Runtime Admission, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to executable domain and interface assurance suite.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-4
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Well-typed clients make server validation redundant. This fails the Runtime Admission contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Defend the Runtime Admission model · Theory Runtime Admission
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Runtime Admission: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Schemas and endpoints look complete while identity, authorization, concurrency, numerical, lifecycle, compatibility, and recovery invariants remain implicit.
